notes: Staged LLM extraction directly from each source span. Consumes only the span text and declared source metadata; never the manual object set or expected_* labels. Review priority is assigned application-side by type. Prompt version: v1.

predictions.predicted_statement: New clients must pay the standard deposit before scheduling can be finalized, unless an approved exception applies.
After-hours scheduling generally needs operations lead approval, except for emergency priority incidents.
When active water damage, loss of heat, or another safety issue is present, dispatch may treat the situation as an emergency and bypass the usual after‑hours approval requirement.
VIP maintenance clients may bypass the usual deposit requirement when the dispatcher confirms the account and records the justification.
Before confirming a job, check if a documented exception applies and record the waiver reason in the job record.
Emergency dispatch must classify the incident and verify technician availability.
Clients with an active maintenance plan and a recent service visit are considered long-term maintenance clients.
The VIP client list is the internal roster of long‑term maintenance accounts that receive deposit flexibility and expedited handling.
Emergency priority applies only to active damage, safety hazards, or service outages that affect core habitability.

predictions.predicted_title: Notify field lead when tech unavailable
predictions.predicted_statement: The dispatcher must notify the field lead if no qualified technician is available for a same‑day emergency request.
Deposit exceptions must be recorded in the job record prior to confirming scheduling.
If an emergency request lacks a qualified technician, field‑lead reassignment approval must be obtained before promising same‑day service.
Effective immediately, only accounts with an active maintenance plan may receive VIP deposit waivers.
During after‑hours intake, verify that the issue qualifies as an emergency before seeking operations lead approval.
After‑hours calls require operations lead approval unless the outage affects core habitability.
